ground_truth_summary: PA chest X-ray with a rib fracture on the LEFT 10th posterior rib (patient's left = right side of displayed image). Fracture appears as a lucent line with NO displacement and NO pneumothorax. Costophrenic angles sharp.

judgments.score_cap_reason: Missed the key finding (rib fracture) — major diagnostic error, capped at 4-5 per protocol; further penalized for generic structure-only description with no abnormality mention.
correct_findings: Correctly identified as a chest X-ray
Correctly identified ribs, clavicles, spine, lungs, heart, diaphragm
Correctly noted no visible text/labels
incorrect_findings: FAILED to detect the rib fracture on the left 10th posterior rib — the primary diagnostic finding
Claimed 'artifacts' are dust particles without exploring for actual pathology
Mentioned liver on wrong side (said 'left side of image = patient's right' is correct, but called it liver when describing below diaphragm)
hallucinations: Described 'tiny bright white specks scattered across the image, likely dust particles' — not a clinically relevant observation
missing_findings: Rib fracture on left 10th posterior rib (patient's left, right side of displayed image)
judgments.clinical_usefulness: Low — describes anatomy but misses the only clinically actionable finding. A radiologist relying on this description would miss a rib fracture.
judgments.response_quality_notes: Well-structured anatomical description but no clinical analysis. Did not look for or mention fractures, pathology, or abnormalities.

judgments.score_cap_reason: Missed the key finding (rib fracture) — capped at 4-5 per protocol; score in lower range due to no clinical abnormality mention at all.
correct_findings: Correctly identified as chest X-ray (PA/AP projection)
Correctly identified ribs, clavicles, spine, lungs, heart, diaphragm
Correctly noted no pneumothorax (incidentally true)
Correctly noted cardiothoracic ratio within normal limits
incorrect_findings: FAILED to detect the rib fracture on the left 10th posterior rib — primary diagnostic finding
Stated 'no obvious signs of acute consolidation or pneumothorax' without examining the ribs for fracture
missing_findings: Rib fracture on left 10th posterior rib
Statement that ribs were 'clearly visible' but did not assess integrity
judgments.clinical_usefulness: Low — clean description of normal anatomy but missed the fracture entirely.
judgments.response_quality_notes: Better-structured than gemma4-12b, with explicit mention of pneumothorax absence, but still completely missed the fracture.

judgments.score_cap_reason: Missed the key finding (rib fracture) AND made an explicit false-negative statement ('no obvious fractures or dislocations'). Major diagnostic error capped at 4-5; lower score due to explicit false negative.
correct_findings: Correctly identified as chest X-ray
Correctly identified skeletal and thoracic structures
Correctly noted no consolidation, pleural effusion, or masses
Correctly noted normal hemidiaphragm position (right slightly higher)
incorrect_findings: EXPLICIT FALSE NEGATIVE: 'The ribs and clavicles are well-defined with no obvious fractures or dislocations' — the rib fracture IS present
FAILED to detect the rib fracture on the left 10th posterior rib
missing_findings: Rib fracture on left 10th posterior rib (lucent line)
Assessment of rib integrity was performed and reported as normal, which is incorrect
judgments.clinical_usefulness: Very low — actively misleads by explicitly stating no fractures are present when one is. A clinician relying on this could discharge a patient with an undiagnosed rib fracture.
judgments.response_quality_notes: Most harmful response of the group because it explicitly rules out the fracture that is present.

judgments.score_cap_reason: Missed the key finding (rib fracture) AND made explicit false-negative statements about fracture absence. Major diagnostic error capped at 4-5; lower score for multiple false reassurances.
correct_findings: Correctly identified as chest X-ray (frontal/AP)
Correctly identified ribs, spine, clavicles, lungs, heart, diaphragm
incorrect_findings: EXPLICIT FALSE NEGATIVE: 'The ribs are clearly visible, with no signs of fractures or deformities'
EXPLICIT FALSE NEGATIVE: 'no visible abnormalities or pathologies' (stated twice)
FAILED to detect the rib fracture on the left 10th posterior rib
Hallucinated a 'sternum visible as central vertical white line' — the sternum is not well-visualized on a PA chest X-ray in this manner
hallucinations: Claimed sternum visible as 'central, vertical white line running down the middle of the chest, connecting the ribs' — incorrect for PA chest X-ray
Claimed trachea visible as 'central, vertical line running down the middle of the chest' — overstated visibility
missing_findings: Rib fracture on left 10th posterior rib (lucent line)
Any mention of abnormality despite explicit pathology check
judgments.clinical_usefulness: Very low — explicitly normal report on an abnormal film, plus anatomical hallucinations.
judgments.response_quality_notes: Confidently incorrect. Worst combination: missed the real finding and asserted normalcy while hallucinating anatomical details.

judgments.score_cap_reason: Missed the key finding (rib fracture) AND made explicit false-negative statements. Major diagnostic error capped at 4-5; lower score for confident false reassurance.
correct_findings: Correctly identified as PA chest X-ray
Correctly identified ribs, sternum, spine, clavicles, scapulae, lungs, heart, diaphragm, costophrenic angles
Correctly noted no consolidation/infiltrates, normal heart size, normal diaphragm level
incorrect_findings: EXPLICIT FALSE NEGATIVE: 'The ribs and spine appear to be intact, with no obvious fractures or deformities' — rib fracture IS present
EXPLICIT FALSE NEGATIVE: 'appears to be of a healthy individual, with no obvious signs of disease or pathology'
FAILED to detect the rib fracture on the left 10th posterior rib
missing_findings: Rib fracture on left 10th posterior rib (lucent line)
Specific rib integrity assessment was performed and incorrectly reported as normal
judgments.clinical_usefulness: Very low — explicitly normal report on abnormal film.
judgments.response_quality_notes: More detailed than qwen3-vl-4b but equally wrong; explicit false negative on the fracture.

judgments.score_cap_reason: Missed the key finding (rib fracture) — capped at 4-5 per protocol. Higher end of cap because it noted a faint opacity in the correct anatomical region (viewer's right lower lung field) near where the fracture is located, and avoided a hard false-negative on fracture absence.
correct_findings: Correctly identified as chest X-ray (PA/AP)
Correctly identified skeletal and thoracic structures

Noted 'faint, patchy opacities (whiter areas) visible in the lower left lung field (viewer's right), near the diaphragm' — this region is in the correct area (viewer's right = patient's left = fracture location)
Correctly noted costophrenic angles sharp (no pleural effusion)
Correctly noted heart slightly to viewer's right (patient's left)
incorrect_findings: FAILED to explicitly detect the rib fracture on the left 10th posterior rib
Stated 'The visible bones appear intact with no obvious fractures or dislocations' — though less confident than Qwen3-VL siblings
Speculated the opacity was 'vascular markings, breast shadowing, or potentially early consolidation' rather than recognizing it could relate to rib pathology
missing_findings: Explicit identification of the lucent fracture line on the 10th posterior rib
Connection between the noted opacity region and possible rib abnormality
judgments.clinical_usefulness: Moderate — best of the group because it noted an abnormal finding in the correct region, even though it did not characterize it as a rib fracture. A clinician might investigate further based on the opacity mention.
judgments.response_quality_notes: Only model to mention an abnormal finding in the fracture region. Did not explicitly rule out fractures with high confidence ('no OBVIOUS fractures'). Most cautious and closest-to-correct response.

summary.best_response: qwen3.5-4b-Q8|think (score 4) — noted an opacity in the correct anatomical region near the fracture
summary.worst_response: glm-4.6v-flash-9b-Q8|think and qwen3-vl-4b-Q8|think (score 2) — explicit confident false negatives with hallucinations
summary.common_pattern: All models describe normal anatomy well but fail to detect rib fractures — a known VLM weakness on subtle linear lucencies in radiographs.
